title: Mandatory Publishing, Returns, Alternate Exchanges, and Route Audits · Transfer Build
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers mandatory publishing, returns, alternate exchanges, and route audits into a new unroutable publication detection and ownership record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of unroutable publication detection and ownership record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Register return handling before mandatory publication and correlate returned content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.
Distinguish broker confirms from consumer processing and combine mandatory returns or alternate routing with confirm results.
Build topology fixtures that assert expected destination sets, forbidden routes, unroutable cases, permissions, and changed bindings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Mandatory Return Sequence
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 6339 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep mandatory return, alternate custody, confirmation, and route uncertainty separate; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Register return handling before mandatory publication and correlate returned content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Mandatory Return Sequence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Alternate Return Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Alternate Return Tradeoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab messaging team handles case 2910 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep mandatory return, alternate custody, confirmation, and route uncertainty separate; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Alternate Return Tradeoff.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Alternate Return Tradeoff, keep Alternate Return Tradeoff connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.
